question: The scatter plot shows CO₂ emissions vs. temperature anomaly. Which statement is most consistent with the data in the chart?

options.A: Temperature anomalies tend to increase as CO₂ emissions increase.
options.B: Higher CO₂ emissions are associated with lower temperature anomalies.
options.C: There is no visible relationship between CO₂ emissions and temperature anomaly.
options.D: The points with the largest size and darkest color show that temperature anomalies stay roughly constant as CO₂ emissions rise.

explanation: This scatter plot uses dual encoding: point position shows the relationship, but point size and color also encode the x-variable, making high values overly salient. That can tempt viewers to overstate the strength or form of the relationship.
